method: gated ingestion of hybrid-discovery top-80 (by sem_score); measures accept rate + how many ACCEPTED pmcids are keyword-MISSED (absent from data/corpus/incoming/organoid_corpus_candidates_{180,generated,gen2,gen3}.csv) -- i.e. the saturation-breaker hypothesis tested end-to-end
hypothesis: saturation-breaker: semantic discovery surfaces QC-passing protocol papers that keyword discovery missed
finding: NEGATIVE result. Of 10 QC-accepted papers, 0 were keyword-missed: all 10 already appear in the keyword candidate pools (180/generated/gen2/gen3, n=1306), so a keyword+QC pipeline would also have found them. Two effects combine: (1) hybrid_discover dedups only against corpus + the 180-pool, NOT gen2/gen3, so it re-surfaces keyword-discoverable papers; (2) the genuinely keyword-missed high-sem papers are review-heavy and QC-rejected (no_signaling). Conclusion at this corpus scale: semantic discovery's value is ranking/recall ORDERING, not net-new primary protocol papers -- keyword discovery already covers the QC-passing protocol papers. Recommendation: keep semantic for re-ranking/QC support; do not chase it for corpus growth; fix hybrid_discover dedup to exclude gen2/gen3 before any future keyword-missed claim. The +10 papers were NOT added to the corpus (not novel); this PR ships the measurement only.
